label: Uncomplicated body ringworm without cellulitis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized tinea corporis or body ringworm.
No cellulitis, abscess, scalp/nail/beard involvement, extensive disease, or immunocompromised-host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Cellulitis, abscess, or secondary infection concern.
Scalp, nail, beard, groin, or foot fungal pathway requiring different instructions.
Extensive, recurrent, treatment-failure, or immunocompromised-host disease.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if the rash is not improving with treatment or if it returns.

source_audit.notes: CDC supports ringworm and tinea framing, contagious spread precautions, antifungal treatment concepts, and special handling for scalp, nail, extensive, persistent, recurrent, or high-risk disease. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized body ringworm without cellulitis, abscess, scalp/nail/beard/groin/foot pathway, systemic illness, treatment failure, or immunocompromised-host pathway.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. CDC supports ringworm and tinea framing, contagious spread precautions, antifungal treatment concepts, and special handling for scalp, nail, extensive, persistent, recurrent, or high-risk disease. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized body ringworm without cellulitis, abscess, scalp/nail/beard/groin/foot pathway, systemic illness, treatment failure, or immunocompromised-host pathway.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized tinea corporis or body ringworm
localized skin-only disease and no cellulitis or abscess documented
no scalp, nail, beard, groin, foot, recurrent, extensive, or immunocompromised-host pathway
